
| Parameter | Nimesa | Appranix | Comments | 
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cost | Quote: $35 | Quote $25 | 
| Ease of Use | We have to do the setup of Nimesa VMs, We had to give the replacement instance types. Maintaining the nimesa VM would be on us| Setup of backups was easy. Since it is a Saas solution we don't have to manage the setup or upgrades |
| Features | Nimesa supports EKS migration, but we faced many issues while doing the migration during PoC. Route53 is not supported yet. | Appranix does not directly support migration of EKS cluster, need EKS cluster to be pre created. EKS does not support post hooks. | Both the solutions have hooks for EC2, from where we can trigger scripts
| Security | The data would reside only in our VPC | The metadata would be stored with Appranix which could actually help during ransomware attacks |
| Time To Restore | Can migrate 150Vms at once | --- |
| AWS Services Supported | EC2, EKS, RDS, LBs | EC2, Route53, RDS, LBs|
| --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- |

Points to consider during Implementations
- Script to change the ip address of NAT Gateways
- Active Directory Setup in Hyd Region
- Enable ECR multi region
- Check for SQS, ECR, SNS, Lambda setup?
- 

Questions for Nimesa
- Do they support ALB/NLB creation?
- Do they support 
- How to revert from DR to main site
- What is the min time that can be set?
- Support
Questions for Appranix

- Backups are crash consistent or app consistent?


|   |   |   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|**Criteria's**|**Nimesa**|**Appranix**|**Datamotive**|**Amazon DRS**|**Comments**|
|Cost|Following is the cost estimate that was shared by Nimesa based on our Prod account. The price for EC2 with DB is $35, and without it is $25.  <br>![Screenshot 2023-12-26 at 4.26.08 PM.png](data:image/png;base64,)|The cost shared by Appranix is $25 per instance.  <br>Will try to get the exact quote for our env.|The cost shared by Datanotive was $30 per instance.|AWS DRS charges $20 per instance.|As appranix and nimesa both work on snapshots, we won’t be requireing our existing snapshots which would be a cost saving for us.|
|Security|Nimesa stores both the data and metadata inside our AWS account.  <br>One disadvantage of this approach could be, what if the AWS account itself gets compromised due to security breach.|Appranix stores data inside our AWS account, but the metadata is stored in their DC.  <br>This can be helpful to restore to a different account in case our AWS account is compromised.|Datamotive stores data inside our AWS Account.|||
|Supported Features  <br>( AWS )|See the below table|See the below table||graviton is not supported, since most of our dbs are based on graviton we won’t be doing a complete PoC for DRS.||
|Fallback ( Mumbai )|This is not yet tested|This is not yet tested|This is not yet tested|||
|Reports and monitoring|No reports are generated|Reports can be generated once a DR drill is done, also we can setup alerts in case some replication is not working properly.|No reports are generated|||
|Ease of Use|Nimesa is not very user friendly because:  <br><br>1. We will have to manage the application i.e upgrades patching of the nimesa VM.<br>2. UI was also not user friendly|1. Appranix UI felt better during the PoC.|Not very user friendly. Have lot of issues in Navigation|||
|Team Support|1. Nimesa team would help us setup the DR and will prioritize any feature requirenment|1. Appranix team would help us setup the DR and will prioritise any feature requirement|1. Appranix team would help us setup the DR and will prioritize any feature requirenment|||
|Triggering DR|1. Triggering DR is straighforward, but faced issues while doing the DR.<br>2. Have to provide the mapping of the instance type not available in Hyd region<br>3. During PoC the replication of EKS didn’t work as expected.|1. Triggering DR is straighforward, but the contents of Hosted Zones was not replicated when we tried replication for the first time. Which was fixed later on.||||
|Min Protection Policy|1. 15 min|1. 15 mins||||
|Cross DR Support (  <br>Multi Cloud and On-Prem )|1. AWS and Azure are supported. Cross cloud replication is not supported.|1. Multiple clouds are suppoted, but cross cloud replication is not|1. Multiple and cross could replication is supported|||
|User Management|1. Can add and create new user.|1. Can add and create new user.<br>2. MFA is also supported.|1. Can add and create new user.|||
|Control plane Architecture|1. In case of a disaster the Nimesa VM becomes a single point of failure for the recovery to happen successfully.<br>2. It is an air gapped solution<br>3. Small cost would be incurred to run the Nimesa EC2 instance|1. Its a SaaS model.<br>2. We can only take the backup of the metadata without the actual data.||||
|Customisation|Supported|Supported|Supported|||
|Discovery|Auto discovered the resporces|Auto discovered the resporces|Auto discovery not supported|||


|   |   |   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|**Services**|**Nimesa**|**Appranix**|**Datamotive**|**Amazon DRS**|**Comments**|
|EC2|Linux ( amd64 ) : Supported  <br>Linux ( arm64 ) : Supported  <br>Windows : Did not tested|Linux ( amd64 ) : Supported  <br>Linux ( arm64 ) : Supported  <br>Windows : Did not tested|Linux ( amd64 ) : Supported  <br>Linux ( arm64 ) : Supported  <br>Windows : Did not tested|graviton is not supported, since most of our dbs are based on graviton we won’t be doing a complete PoC for DRS.||
|EKS|Replicating EKS cluster is supported which did not work during the PoC|Replicating the deployments of EKS is supported. Have not tried yet. Ideally this is the feature that we would require.|Need landing zone to be created. Have not tested much on creating applications in DR Region|||
|Route53|Not Supported. Will have to be managed by writing custom scripts.|Supported (Did not work in the first instance). It does migrate the entire Route53 in one go.|Not Supported Directly, post hooks can be used|||
|VPC|Supported, we will have to manage creating the AWS VPN connections with our DC|Supported, we will have to manage creating the AWS VPN connections with our DC. Might have to write a script to migrate the Elastic IP from one DC to another.|Supported|||
|ELB|ELB is supported|ELB is supported|ELB is supported|||
|Redshift, ECR, SNS, SES|Will have to check the multi region support in AWS itself|Will have to check the multi region support in AWS itself|Will have to check the multi region support in AWS itself|||
